tasks.name: Save two landscape paintings from 2022 or later under $300 to favorites
tasks.description: Navigate the gallery, filter for landscape works created in 2022 or later and priced under $300, sort by price, and save the first two results to the favorites list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Gallery' link in the main navigation menu.
In the gallery filters, click the 'Category' dropdown and select 'Landscape'.
Click the 'Year' filter and set the minimum year to '2022'.
Click the 'Price' filter and enter '300' into the maximum price field.
Click the 'Apply filters' button to update the gallery results.
Click the sort dropdown above the gallery and select 'Price: Low to High'.
Click the 'Add to Favorites' button on the first artwork in the filtered list, then click the 'Add to Favorites' button on the second artwork.
Click the 'Favorites' icon or link in the header to open the favorites page and view the saved artworks.

tasks.name: Add the most recent cityscape as a large framed print to the cart
tasks.description: Find the newest cityscape print, select the large 60 x 90 cm size with a black frame, and add one copy to the shopping cart.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Shop Prints' link in the main navigation menu.
In the shop sidebar, click the 'Category' section and select 'Cityscapes'.
At the top of the product grid, open the sort dropdown and choose 'Newest First'.
Click the image or title of the first cityscape print in the sorted results to open its product page.
On the product page, click the 'Size' dropdown and select 'Large 60 x 90 cm'.
In the frame options, select 'Black frame'.
In the quantity field, type '1' if a different value is shown.
Click the 'Add to Cart' button to add the selected large framed print to the cart.

tasks.name: Add three smallest pastel artworks under $200 and max width 50 cm to the wishlist
tasks.description: Filter the gallery for pastel artworks under $200 with maximum width 50 cm, sort by size, and add the three smallest pieces to the wishlist.
steps: From any page, click the site logo to return to the homepage.
Click the 'Gallery' link in the main navigation.
In the filters sidebar, expand the 'Color palette' section and check the 'Pastel' option.
Expand the 'Price' filter and enter '200' in the maximum price field.
Expand the 'Size' filter and set the maximum width field or slider to '50 cm'.
Click the 'Apply filters' button to update the gallery results.
Open the sort dropdown above the gallery and select 'Size: Small to Large'.
Click the 'Add to Wishlist' button on the first artwork, then repeat for the second and third artworks in the sorted list.
Click the 'Wishlist' link or icon in the header to open the wishlist page and view the three saved artworks.

tasks.name: Submit a commission request for a $600 acrylic portrait due on 2025-09-30
tasks.description: Use the commissions page to fill out and submit a custom artwork request for an acrylic portrait with a $600 budget and a preferred completion date of 2025-09-30.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the 'Commissions' link in the main navigation menu.
On the commissions page, click the 'Request a Custom Artwork' button.
In the commission form, open the 'Artwork type' dropdown and select 'Portrait'.
Open the 'Medium' dropdown and select 'Acrylic on canvas'.
In the 'Budget' field, type '600'.
In the 'Preferred completion date' field, enter '2025-09-30'.
In the 'Description' textarea, type a brief subject description such as 'Single-person bust portrait with a neutral background for a living room'.
Scroll to the contact section of the form and type 'Alex Sample' in the 'Name' field and 'alex.sample@example.com' in the 'Email' field.
Click the 'Submit request' button to send the commission request.

tasks.name: Save the first exhibition after 2025-07-01 to the visit plan
tasks.description: Find exhibitions scheduled on or after 2025-07-01, pick the earliest one, and add it to the on-site visit plan list.
steps: From the homepage, click the 'About' link in the main navigation.
On the About page, click the 'Exhibitions & Events' section or tab.
In the exhibitions area, open the date filter and set the 'From' date field to '2025-07-01'.
Click the 'Apply' button on the date filter to show only exhibitions on or after 2025-07-01.
Open the sort dropdown for exhibitions and select 'Date: Soonest First'.
Click the title of the first exhibition in the filtered list to open its details page.
On the exhibition details page, click the 'Add to Visit Plan' or 'Save Event' button.
Click the 'Visit Plan' or 'Saved Events' link in the header or sidebar to open the list of saved exhibitions.

tasks.name: Send a collaboration inquiry referencing a 2023 business mural project
tasks.description: Locate a mural project for a business completed in 2023, capture its title, and submit a collaboration form referencing that project for a new café mural.
steps: From the homepage, click the 'Projects' or 'Case Studies' link in the main navigation.
On the projects page, open the 'Client type' filter and select 'Business / Commercial'.
Open the 'Year' filter and select '2023' as the year.
Click the 'Apply filters' button to update the project list.
Click the title of the first mural project shown in the filtered results to open its project detail page.
On the project detail page, note the full project title, then click the 'Work With Me' or 'Collaboration Inquiry' button.
In the collaboration form, type 'Jordan Lee' into the 'Name' field and 'jordan.lee@example.com' into the 'Email' field.
In the 'Project reference' or 'Which project inspired you?' field, type the full title of the mural project you opened.
In the 'Message' textarea, type a short message explaining that you want a similar mural for a café, mentioning an approximate wall size of '4m x 2.5m'.
Click the 'Send message' or 'Submit' button to send the collaboration inquiry.

tasks.name: Subscribe to the newsletter with specific interests and download the 2024 lookbook
tasks.description: Sign up for the artist's newsletter with defined interest categories and frequency, then download the 2024 lookbook PDF from the confirmation page.
steps: On the homepage, scroll to the footer and click the 'Join Newsletter' or 'Stay Updated' button.
In the newsletter signup form, type 'visitor@example.com' into the 'Email' field.
In the 'Interests' checkboxes, select both 'New Prints & Shop Updates' and 'Exhibitions & Events'.
In the 'Email frequency' options, select 'Monthly summary'.
Click the 'Sign Up' or 'Subscribe' button to submit the newsletter form.
On the signup confirmation or thank-you page, locate the 'Download 2024 Lookbook (PDF)' link or button.
Click the 'Download 2024 Lookbook (PDF)' link or button to start downloading the lookbook file.

tasks.name: Add the taller of the first two original artworks under $1000 to the cart
tasks.description: Filter original artworks to those under $1000, compare the height of the first two items, and add whichever is taller to the cart with quantity 1.
steps: From the homepage, click the 'Shop Originals' link in the main navigation.
In the shop filters, open the 'Price' filter and set the maximum price to '1000'.
Click the 'Apply filters' button to update the originals list.
Click the first artwork in the filtered list to open its product details page.
In the details section, read the dimensions and remember the height value in centimeters, then use the 'Back to Originals' link or the browser back button to return to the filtered list.
Click the second artwork in the same filtered list to open its product details page.
In the details section, compare this artwork's height in centimeters with the height you remembered for the first artwork.

If the second artwork is taller, click the 'Add to Cart' button on this page; if it is shorter, use the site's navigation or back button to return to the filtered list and click the first artwork again to open its details page.
On the chosen artwork's product page, type '1' in the quantity field.
Click the 'Add to Cart' button if you have not already added this artwork in step 8.

entities.name: Artwork
entities.description: Portfolio artwork record used in the Gallery, Favorites, Wishlist, and to power some Shop product metadata.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Creation year used for gallery filters like '2022 or later'

fields.name: price
fields.description: Displayed in gallery when applicable; used for price filters like 'under $300' or 'under $200'.

fields.name: is_for_sale
fields.description: Whether the artwork is currently offered for sale.

fields.name: shop_category
fields.description: Links portfolio artwork to a shop category; includes URL param-compatible values 'prints' and 'originals'.

fields.name: additional_image_urls
fields.description: Array of extra image URLs

fields.name: is_featured
fields.description: Used on home page featured artworks sections.

entities.name: Product
entities.description: Purchasable item in the Shop, representing an original artwork or a print configuration.

fields.name: artwork_id
fields.description: Links product to its underlying artwork for shared metadata and imagery.

fields.name: shop_category
fields.description: Matches URL param categoryId=prints|originals and 'Shop Prints' / 'Shop Originals' filters.

fields.name: category
fields.description: Subject/category for shop filters like 'Cityscapes'.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Year used for 'Newest First' sorting.

fields.name: price
fields.description: Base/list price used in price filters (e.g., under $1000).

fields.name: available
fields.description: Whether the item can currently be added to cart.

fields.name: stock_quantity
fields.description: For originals typically 1; for prints can be higher or null for unlimited.

fields.name: additional_image_urls
fields.description: Array of extra image URLs

fields.name: width_cm
fields.description: Dimensions as shown on product detail and used in comparisons like 'taller of the first two'.

fields.name: size_options
fields.description: Array of size option objects, e.g., [{code, label:'Large 60 x 90 cm', width_cm, height_cm, price}].

fields.name: frame_options
fields.description: Array of frame option objects, e.g., [{code, label:'Black frame', price_delta}].

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Shopping cart (single cart for the agent) holding items to purchase.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Array of CartItem IDs or embedded items for quick access.

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Line item in the cart, with selected configuration such as size, frame, and quantity.

fields.name: artwork_id
fields.description: Optional: for easier linking back to the artwork/gallery card.

fields.name: unit_price
fields.description: Price per unit at the time of adding to cart, including configuration adjustments.

fields.name: selected_size_label
fields.description: E.g., 'Large 60 x 90 cm'.

fields.name: selected_frame_label
fields.description: E.g., 'Black frame'.

entities.name: FavoritesList
entities.description: Single favorites collection for artworks the visitor has marked as favorites.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name, e.g., 'Favorites'.

fields.name: item_ids
fields.description: Array of FavoriteItem IDs for this list.

entities.name: FavoriteItem
entities.description: Single artwork saved into the favorites list.

entities.name: Wishlist
entities.description: Wishlist collection for artworks and products the visitor is considering.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name, e.g., 'Wishlist'.

fields.name: item_ids
fields.description: Array of WishlistItem IDs.

entities.name: WishlistItem
entities.description: Individual entry in the wishlist, referencing either a gallery artwork or a shop product.

fields.name: artwork_id
fields.description: Set when adding from Gallery.

fields.name: product_id
fields.description: Set when adding directly from a product detail page.

entities.name: CommissionRequest
entities.description: Submitted commission/custom artwork requests from the Commissions page.

fields.name: artwork_type
fields.description: E.g., 'Portrait' as in the request form.

fields.name: medium
fields.description: Includes 'acrylic_on_canvas' as used in task 4.

entities.name: Exhibition
entities.description: Exhibitions and events listed under 'Exhibitions & Events' and on the exhibition detail page.

fields.name: start_date
fields.description: Used for filters like 'From 2025-07-01' and 'Date: Soonest First' sorting.

entities.name: VisitPlan
entities.description: Single visit plan list containing exhibitions/events the visitor has saved.

fields.name: item_ids
fields.description: Array of VisitPlanItem IDs.

entities.name: VisitPlanItem
entities.description: An exhibition saved into the Visit Plan (Saved Events).

entities.name: Project
entities.description: Client projects / case studies (e.g., business murals) shown on the Projects and Project Detail pages.

fields.name: client_type
fields.description: Includes 'business_commercial' as used by the Client type filter.

fields.name: project_category
fields.description: Allows selecting mural projects for tasks like the business mural example.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Used in Year filter (e.g., 2023).

fields.name: wall_width_m
fields.description: Optional: wall dimensions for mural projects.

entities.name: CollaborationInquiry
entities.description: Messages sent via the collaboration / 'Work With Me' form, optionally referencing a specific project.

fields.name: project_reference_id
fields.description: ID of the project that inspired the inquiry, when coming from a project detail page.

fields.name: project_reference_title
fields.description: Full project title typed or prefilled (used explicitly in task 6).

fields.name: message
fields.description: Free-text inquiry, may include details like 'similar mural for a café, 4m x 2.5m wall'.

entities.name: NewsletterSubscription
entities.description: Newsletter signup records with email, frequency preference, and metadata.

fields.name: frequency
fields.description: Includes 'monthly_summary' as in the signup form.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Where the user signed up from.

fields.name: lookbook_2024_downloaded
fields.description: Whether the 2024 lookbook PDF has been downloaded from the thank-you page.

entities.name: NewsletterSubscriptionInterest
entities.description: Join table representing each interest category selected for a newsletter subscription (supports multi-select interests).

fields.name: interest_code
fields.description: Includes 'new_prints_shop_updates' and 'exhibitions_events' selected in task 7.

entities.name: DocumentAsset
entities.description: Generic document/media assets like the 2024 lookbook PDF, press kits, etc.

fields.name: document_category
fields.description: The lookbook uses category 'lookbook'.

fields.name: file_type
fields.description: The 2024 lookbook is a PDF.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Used to identify the '2024 Lookbook'.
